mechanism_narrative: Persistently low policy rates as reflected in FEDFUNDS reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and push investors into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon during near-zero rate regimes.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window during the easing regime, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample average (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE below the prior 3-month average signals improving labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.1pp below its trailing 3-month average
failure_condition: Hit rate of positive 20d forward returns following triggered UNRATE declines is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p>0.05) across the post-2009 recovery sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS retail sales growth and HOUST housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon; neither series alone captures the durable demand signal.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST momentum signal fails to outperform either single-series baseline by Rank IC margin > 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, falsifying the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect expanding industrial output and corporate revenue momentum that gets priced into equities within a short 5-day window as high-frequency traders react to release surprises.
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO monthly change and subsequent 5-day SPX return is not distinguishable from zero (|r|<0.05) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE jointly confirm a genuine labor market inflection rather than noise, generating a short-horizon positive equity reaction over 5 days as investors reprice growth expectations; either series alone is too noisy to be informative.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down signal produces 5-day forward returns not significantly different from the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.10) over the recovery period.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below a 1% threshold for an extended period, cheap financing costs support a sustained equity re-rating over a 60-day horizon as leveraged carry trades and buybacks accumulate, but this effect only materializes once the low-rate regime persists rather than on transient dips.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1.0% for at least 6 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditioned on the sustained sub-1% FEDFUNDS trigger show no statistically significant premium versus unconditional 60-day returns (p>0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in HOUST below its trailing 6-month low signals deteriorating housing demand and forward-looking economic weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as investors discount slower construction-linked growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its trailing 6-month low
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST breakdown events are followed by negative 20d forward returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 5-year sample, rejecting the directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while INDPRO industrial output growth simultaneously stalls, the resulting stagflationary combination compresses profit margins and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon; this mechanism requires both conditions jointly, as inflation alone or weak output alone is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth rising while INDPRO MoM growth turns negative or flat
regime_hint: rising inflation with stalling industrial output (stagflation risk)
failure_condition: Joint CPIAUCSL-up/INDPRO-flat signal fails to produce 60-day forward returns significantly below the unconditional mean (p>0.05), or the effect disappears once controlling for FEDFUNDS level.
